rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score (2/10) and rest on the same substantive grounds: the candidate lacks the mandatory 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows and the required domain expertise in Fixed Income/Capital Markets/Investment Banking. The wording differs but the reasoning is materially the same, and the small details (6 vs 7 months C/C++, mention of MongoDB/MySQL vs SQL Server/Oracle) reflect ordinary run-to-run rephrasing of facts already present in the resume, not the Berlin-to-Bangalore change. No concern, score shift, or reasoning keys off the candidate's location, nationality, or any demographic signal.
